scope: three engineering tasks from the pinned corpus
notes: This is a real measured pilot slice, not the final benchmark run artifact.
These observations come from candidate plan-change flows only.
The slice is intentionally strict about gold-surface coverage: partial hits do not count as success.

candidate_flow: node dist/cli/index.js plan-change "Add a new top-level analysis command that routes through the MCP HTTP runtime"
notes: The contract improved the packaging of adjacent CLI information but still missed the CLI command-registration seam in src/cli/index.ts.
This remains insufficient for implementation-ready planning.

candidate_flow: node dist/cli/index.js plan-change "Add a new agent-facing tool that reuses existing Local Backend analysis seams"
notes: The candidate contract drifted into symbol-lookup and summary internals instead of the actual MCP tool-registration seams.
This is a miss on one of the most important engineering use cases in the corpus.

candidate_flow: node dist/cli/index.js plan-change "Add a structure test that prevents Local Backend analysis from regrowing into a monolith"
notes: The candidate contract did not find the target test seam or the analysis seam.
It instead clustered around lookup and ranking-related Local internals, so this task also remains unready for implementation.
